name: Oatmeal Blueberry Bread
method: set the oven to 370 degrees Fahrenheit. Spray or gently grease a
9-inch loaf pan with baking spray or canola oil, line with
parchment paper, or lightly butter the pan and sprinkle with
Combine the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large
mixing basin. Combine the yogurt, eggs, melted butter, and
vanilla in a separate dish with a fork, then stir into the dry
ingredients. Combine in the oats, then mix in the blueberries
carefully, reserving a few to sprinkle across the top of the batter.
It's best if the batter is thick and a bit sticky.
Scrape the batter into the loaf pan using a spatula. Bake for 45-
55 minutes, or until golden brown and a toothpick inserted into
the center of the bread comes out clean. Erase from the oven and
cool for 10 minutes in the pan before transferring to a wire rack
to cool completely. Warm or at room temperature is OK. After
the bread has cooled thoroughly, it will be firmer.
